prompt:
Summarize the following meeting notes in 3-4 bullet points:

Project Alpha Status Meeting - March 20, 2026
Attendees: Maria (PM), Luca (Dev Lead), Sara (QA), Roberto (Design)

Maria opened by reviewing the timeline. Sprint 14 is 2 days behind due to API integration delays with the third-party payment provider. Luca explained that the provider changed their authentication flow from OAuth to mTLS with only 3 days notice. The team has adapted but needs 2 more days for testing.

Sara raised concerns about regression testing coverage - currently at 72%, target is 85%. She requested 1 additional QA resource for the next 2 sprints. Maria agreed to escalate to the PMO.

Roberto presented the updated mobile UI mockups. The team approved the new dashboard design but requested changes to the notification panel - too many icons, needs simplification. Roberto will deliver revised mockups by Friday.

Action items: Luca to complete payment integration by March 24. Sara to create regression test plan. Roberto to revise notification panel. Maria to request QA resource from PMO.
system_prompt: You are an executive assistant. Summarize meeting notes concisely, capturing key decisions, blockers, and action items.
expected_output:
• **Timeline**: Sprint 14 is 2 days behind due to third-party payment provider switching from OAuth to mTLS with minimal notice; completion expected by March 24.
• **QA Coverage**: Regression testing at 72% (target 85%); Maria to escalate request for 1 additional QA resource to PMO.
• **Design**: New mobile dashboard approved; notification panel needs simplification — revised mockups due Friday.
• **Action items**: Luca → payment integration (Mar 24), Sara → regression test plan, Roberto → revised mockups (Friday), Maria → PMO resource request.

prompt:
Summarize this email thread:

From: Sarah Chen
To: James Walker, Priya Sharma
Subject: RE: Model Retirement Timeline

Team,

Following up on our discussion with the enterprise customer. They've raised three main concerns:
1. Building test sets is nearly impossible for their use cases
2. Out-of-the-box evaluators won't work for their domain-specific needs
3. The overall process is too complex and expensive

I think we need to address these point by point in Thursday's call. James, can you prepare the technical demo? Priya, please check if we have any case studies from similar enterprise migrations.

Also worth noting: their system integrator has already submitted a proposal for this work. We should be aware of what they're quoting.

---
From: James Walker
To: Sarah Chen, Priya Sharma
Re: RE: Model Retirement Timeline

Sarah, I'll prepare a live demo showing the evaluation pipeline. I can show:
- Stored completions for automated test set creation
- LLM-as-judge for domain-agnostic evaluation
- Effort comparison showing it doesn't require a massive project

Will need access to a test deployment by Wednesday.

---
From: Priya Sharma
To: Sarah Chen, James Walker

I found 2 relevant case studies from telecom and banking sectors. Both completed migration in under 2 weeks. Will share the decks.

Also flagged: the customer's standard deployments are already auto-upgrading since the latest retirement cycle. This adds urgency.
system_prompt: You are an executive assistant. Summarize email threads highlighting decisions, action items, and key information.
expected_output:
**Summary**: Enterprise customer has raised 3 objections to model migration (test set creation, evaluator customization, complexity). Thursday call planned to address these.

**Key intel**: Their system integrator has already submitted a proposal; standard deployments are auto-upgrading (adds urgency).

**Action items**: James → prepare live demo (stored completions, LLM-as-judge, effort comparison) — needs test deployment by Wednesday. Priya → share 2 case studies (telecom + banking, both <2 weeks migration).

prompt:
Summarize the following technical document in one paragraph:

Azure OpenAI v1 API Migration Guide

The v1 API introduces several breaking changes from the legacy API. Authentication switches from API keys to Azure Entra ID (recommended) or API keys via Bearer tokens. The base URL changes from {resource}.openai.azure.com/openai/deployments/{deployment} to {resource}.openai.azure.com/openai/v1. Client initialization uses the standard OpenAI SDK with base_url parameter instead of AzureOpenAI. The max_tokens parameter is renamed to max_completion_tokens. Reasoning models (GPT-5.1, o-series) replace the system role with developer role and don't support temperature/top_p parameters — use reasoning_effort instead. Structured outputs remain compatible. Tool calling schemas are unchanged but models may make different routing decisions. Response format supports json_schema as before. Migration testing should cover: authentication, parameter naming, role handling, and output quality.
system_prompt: You are a technical writer. Create clear, accurate summaries that preserve essential technical details.
expected_output: The Azure OpenAI v1 API migration involves several breaking changes: authentication shifts to Entra ID with Bearer tokens, the base URL moves to the /openai/v1 path, and clients use the standard OpenAI SDK with base_url instead of AzureOpenAI. Key parameter changes include max_tokens → max_completion_tokens, and reasoning models (GPT-5.1, o-series) use the developer role instead of system, drop temperature/top_p in favor of reasoning_effort. Structured outputs and tool calling schemas remain compatible, though model routing decisions may differ. Testing should cover authentication, parameter naming, role handling, and output quality.

prompt:
Provide a 2-sentence summary of this news article:

Rome, March 2026 — Energia Italia SpA announced today a €2.1 billion investment in renewable energy infrastructure across Southern Italy and North Africa. The plan includes 3 GW of solar capacity in Sicily and Sardinia, two offshore wind farms in the Mediterranean, and a green hydrogen pilot plant in Libya. CEO Marco Alberti stated the investment reflects the company's commitment to achieving net-zero emissions by 2050. The projects are expected to create 12,000 jobs and generate first power by 2028. Environmental groups have cautiously welcomed the move but noted the company's continued investment in fossil fuel exploration in the same regions. The Italian government will co-fund 15% of the solar projects through its National Recovery Plan.
system_prompt: You are a news summarizer. Be concise and factual. Capture the most important information.
expected_output: Energia Italia SpA is investing €2.1 billion in renewable energy across Southern Italy and North Africa, including 3 GW of solar, offshore wind farms, and a green hydrogen pilot plant, targeting first power by 2028. The investment, supported by 15% Italian government co-funding, aims to create 12,000 jobs as part of the company's net-zero 2050 commitment.

prompt:
Summarize the key findings from this incident report:

Incident Report IR-2026-0342
Date: March 15, 2026
Severity: P1 (Critical)
Duration: 4 hours 23 minutes (06:17 - 10:40 UTC)

Description: Complete outage of the customer-facing API gateway affecting all European regions. Root cause was a misconfigured rate limiting rule deployed at 06:15 UTC that blocked all traffic instead of limiting to 1000 req/s. The rule was part of a scheduled maintenance update that bypassed the staging environment due to an emergency change process.

Impact: ~45,000 API calls failed. 312 enterprise customers affected. Estimated revenue impact: €180,000. SLA breach for 28 customers.

Resolution: On-call engineer identified the issue at 07:02. Rollback initiated at 07:15 but failed due to missing rollback configuration. Manual fix applied at 10:30, service restored at 10:40.

Root Cause: (1) Rate limiting rule had regex error matching all paths instead of /api/v2/*. (2) Emergency change process skipped staging validation. (3) Rollback configuration was not maintained.

Action Items: (1) Mandate staging for all changes, including emergency. (2) Implement automated rollback testing. (3) Add rate-limit rule unit tests. (4) Review emergency change process.
system_prompt: You are an SRE analyst. Summarize incident reports highlighting impact, root cause, and corrective actions.
expected_output: **Incident IR-2026-0342 (P1, 4h23m)**: API gateway outage across all European regions caused by a misconfigured rate limiting rule that blocked all traffic. The rule bypassed staging via emergency change process and had a regex error matching all paths. **Impact**: ~45K failed API calls, 312 enterprise customers affected, €180K revenue loss, 28 SLA breaches. Rollback failed due to missing configuration; manual fix took 3+ additional hours. **Corrective actions**: mandatory staging for all changes, automated rollback testing, rate-limit unit tests, emergency process review.

prompt:
Summarize this quarterly business review in 5 bullet points:

Q4 2025 Business Review - GenAI Platform Team

Adoption: Platform usage grew 340% QoQ. Active use cases increased from 12 to 47. Top consumers: Customer Service (35%), Legal (20%), HR (15%), Engineering (30%). Average daily API calls: 2.1M (up from 450K in Q3).

Cost: Total Azure AI spend: €780K/month (up from €260K). Per-use-case cost averaged €16.6K/month. 60% of spend is GPT-4o, 25% embeddings, 15% fine-tuned models. Cost optimization saved €95K through prompt caching and batch API adoption.

Quality: Average user satisfaction: 4.2/5 (up from 3.8). Hallucination rate dropped from 8% to 3% after RAG improvements. Two incidents: one prompt injection attempt (blocked), one PII leak in test environment (contained).

Roadmap: Q1 2026 priorities: (1) Migration to GPT-4.1/5.1 per Azure deprecation timeline, (2) Launch agentic platform pilot with 5 use cases, (3) Implement centralized evaluation framework, (4) Expand to APAC regions.
system_prompt: You are a business analyst. Summarize QBRs concisely, highlighting metrics, trends, and priorities.
expected_output:
• **Adoption surged**: 340% QoQ growth, from 12 to 47 active use cases; daily API calls reached 2.1M (up from 450K).
• **Cost scaling managed**: Azure AI spend at €780K/month (3x QoQ), but cost optimization saved €95K via prompt caching and batch APIs; per-use-case cost: €16.6K/month.
• **Quality improved**: User satisfaction up to 4.2/5; hallucination rate cut from 8% to 3% after RAG improvements.
• **Security**: Two incidents (prompt injection blocked, PII leak in test env contained) — no production data exposure.
• **Q1 2026 priorities**: GPT-4.1/5.1 migration, agentic platform pilot (5 use cases), centralized evaluation framework, APAC expansion.
